Privacy Policy Terms of Service Data Deletion

Data deletion instructions

Last updated: {{docs_date_en}} — {{app_name}} — {{app_id}}

Data Deletion URL (Google Play — Data deletion): {{data_deletion_url}} — public page; no login or submission of personal data is required. Use this exact URL in Play Console (Data safety → Data deletion). Technical: the link is HTTPS; viewing this page does not require cookies from this site and does not rely on a redirect to another domain (use this exact destination URL in the console). The page lets users read data deletion instructions without logging in and without redirects.

This page describes how users can request deletion of their data associated with the app.

We do not store personal data on our own server infrastructure — data is stored exclusively in Google services (Firebase, Google Analytics, AdMob).

You can delete user data stored by the Controller in Firebase services (Authentication and Firestore), to the extent linked to your user account, directly in the app using Account → Delete account — without contacting support.

You may also request deletion of your data from within the app or by email, even if you no longer have access to the device.

The Controller does not keep additional copies of user data outside Google’s services used by the App (including Firebase, Google Analytics, and AdMob); all data deletion operations are performed directly in those services.

Deletion requests are fulfilled through Firebase Authentication and Cloud Firestore mechanisms.

Quick deletion of all data

  • Uninstall the app — removes all local data on the device.
  • Delete account in the app (Account → Delete account) — removes your Firebase / Firestore data held by the Developer.
  • Revoke Google access for the App at myaccount.google.com/permissions — blocks further YouTube API access until you authorise again; it does not delete Firebase account data by itself.

We respect your right to have personal data erased (GDPR Art. 17). {{app_name}} is created by {{full_name}}, a {{legal_status_intro_en}} (developer pseudonym: {{pseudonym}}). Below we explain how you can delete your data.

Third-party providers: Some data may continue to be held by third-party providers in accordance with their own policies (e.g. Google), even after you use the options below.

YouTube Charts: shared aggregated chart metadata in Firestore (the same for everyone) — not personal data about you; no per-account deletion applies.

Google Analytics for Firebase (GA4)

Usage and product events are sent to Google Analytics for Firebase (GA4) when you use the production App. The Developer does not host a separate analytics database. Data is processed by Google; retention and deletion follow Google’s Privacy Policy and Google Analytics / Firebase data settings. You may manage certain activity and account-related controls in your Google Account.

Analytics data is not used by the Controller to directly identify you.

Data are processed in pseudonymised form.

Google Analytics for Firebase applies measures that limit identifiability, including IP address anonymisation in line with Google’s practices.

Local storage (AsyncStorage)

YouTube cache and settings are stored in AsyncStorage only on the device; they are not used by the Controller to identify you (they may include metadata such as video titles or playlists in the cache). Contents are not additionally encrypted at the application level. Data are protected by the operating system’s security mechanisms (app sandbox). Remove them by uninstalling the app or using Clear data in Android settings (see also Option 3).

Option 1: Log out from the App

1 Open {{app_name}}.

2 Open the Account section (Konto in the App; account icon, top right).

3 Log out of YouTube — removes Google/YouTube tokens from the App and ends the YouTube session in the App. Does not automatically delete the video-storage record from device storage.

4 Log out of the App — ends the Firebase session, removes Google/YouTube tokens, and clears visible state in the App. Does not guarantee deletion of the video-storage file from AsyncStorage.

Note: To reliably remove the local copy of your data (including video-storage), use Option 3 (uninstall) or your device’s Clear data / Clear storage for this App.

Option 2: Revoke YouTube OAuth access

1 Open myaccount.google.com/permissions.

2 Find {{app_name}} in the list.

3 Click Remove access to revoke the App’s permissions to your Google/YouTube account.

Option 3: Uninstall the App

Uninstalling the App from an Android device typically removes all locally stored App data (AsyncStorage), including:

  • Cached liked videos and playlists;
  • Locally stored authentication tokens;
  • User preferences (theme, settings);
  • Local ad / ad-free mode data;
  • On-device analytics milestone flags (AsyncStorage keys such as anl_*) used for first-open and retention events.

Option 4: Delete account in the App

1 Open {{app_name}}.

2 Go to the Account section (Konto in the App).

3 Choose Delete account.

4 Confirm the action. Your account and data linked to it in Firebase (Authentication and Firestore) will be permanently deleted.

Deleting your account includes:

  • Account data (email, UID) in Firebase Authentication;
  • Your user data in Firestore (e.g. subscription status);
  • Related identifiers and app data linked to your account.

Warning: Deleting your account is irreversible. You will not be able to recover your data afterwards.

Deletion of data in Firebase takes place promptly after you complete the action, subject to technical delays in Google’s backup systems.

Option 5: Server data deletion request (email)

Alternatively, to delete data held on our backend (Firebase Authentication, Firestore), you can email the Developer at:

Email: {{email}}

Include the email address linked to your App account. We will process the request within 30 days of receipt. The maximum time to respond is 30 days, in accordance with Art. 12(3) GDPR. Please note that after deletion some data may be retained for a limited time in backup copies in accordance with Google Firebase policies.

What happens under each option?

  • Option 1 (log out): OAuth tokens and in-App session end — the video-storage record may remain on the device.
  • Option 2 (revoke OAuth): removes the App’s YouTube API access on Google’s side — does not delete the video-storage file on the device.
  • Option 3 (uninstall / clear app data): typically removes all local App storage (AsyncStorage), including video-storage, local tokens, and preferences.
  • Option 4 (delete account in the App): removes your account data (Firebase Authentication), your user data in Firestore, and related identifiers. Google analytics data may be retained in accordance with Google’s policies.
  • Option 5 (email): Firebase / Firestore account data held by the Developer (account email, display name, UID, subscription metadata) — handled individually per request.

Data processed by third parties (Google AdMob, Google Play Billing, Google Analytics for Firebase (GA4)) is retained according to their policies. See Google’s Privacy Policy. Erasing local App data does not delete all historical analytics data already received by Google’s servers.
